assertionResults.reason: Targeted web and desktop tests both verified single-flight rescan behavior: the controller coalesced overlapping rescans, and the host service reused a single in-flight snapshot request per profile snapshot.
The desktop host safety test proved two rescans left ModsConfig.xml contents and mtime unchanged in a sandboxed config root.
The desktop host safety test proved two rescans created no persistent scan-cache artifacts beyond allowlisted SQLite files in the app-data directory.
The targeted perf test printed 'VAL-HOST-PERF-001 items=2000 warmRescanMs=57.4' and passed, confirming the synthetic 2000-item warm rescan stayed well under the 2000ms threshold.

commandsRun.observation: Vitest passed 10/10 tests, including 'coalesces overlapping rescans into a single host snapshot request', 'preserves active search and source filters when switching profiles', and 'keeps the UI interactive during rescan and preserves search, selection, and feedback'.

bun test passed 3/3 tests, including 'reuses a single in-flight mod source snapshot request per profile snapshot', 'does not modify ModsConfig.xml when rescanning the library', and 'does not create persistent scan cache artifacts under the app data directory'.
bun test passed 2/2 tests and printed 'VAL-HOST-PERF-001 items=2000 warmRescanMs=57.4 cacheHits=2000 cacheMisses=2000 pool=0'.
The live settings resolved to E:\SteamLibrary\steamapps\common\RimWorld plus workshop/config roots, scanned 650 real mods, and logged a warm rescan around 698.8ms with [mod-scan] timing breakdowns.

frictions.description: Task-based flow-validator launch failed immediately because this environment rejects the configured model name custom:GPT-5.4. The parent validator ran the targeted commands directly.
